1.22: Abstain , Reject everything that is evil. From all appearances of evil : of the very appearance of evil, for fear of scandalizing the weak.
1.5: We , Christians, we do not belong to the night, etc.
1.7: In Macedonia and Achaea . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.40: . In Philippi . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.9: . He's the one who will do it. that you may be kept blameless.
1.13: Those who fell asleep, who are dead. See
1.4: The darkness they refer to the unfortunate state of guilty humanity, outside of grace and evangelical truth.
1.19: Do not quench the Spirit , by hindering its operation in you, and preventing those whom he has enriched with his gifts from using them for the benefit of the Church.
1.6: In this matter, that is to say adultery, or other kinds of even more horrible immodesty.
1.21: The prophet receives from God knowledge of the future and sometimes also knowledge of the state of souls; he can read the human heart as if it were a book. Apparitions and particular revelations should not be rejected on principle. Two extremes must be avoided: believing all apparitions without caution or verification, or systematically rejecting them all as impossible, false, or deceitful.
1.23: the mind, the soul and the body , L ’'spirit And blade , In Saint Paul, they do not refer to two distinct substances. The mind , This is the higher part of the soul, the seat of reason, freedom, and the divine life of grace. The spirit is the part of the soul that possesses immaterial intellectual power. The soul gives life to the body; animals also have a soul. Humans are composed of a soul and a body, not of three elements: soul, spirit, and body. The soul is not in man like a hand in a glove; we are a body unified by an immortal soul. Once the soul departs, the body decomposes.

2.1: You yourselves know, brothers, that our coming among you has not been without fruit.
2.2: But after we had suffered and endured insults in Philippi, as you know, God gave us the courage to preach his gospel to you in the midst of many struggles,
2.3: for our preaching did not proceed from error, nor from a vicious intention, nor from any fraud
2.4: But as God has judged us worthy to entrust to us the gospel, so we teach, not to please men, but God, who searches our hearts.
2.5: Indeed, our speeches were never inspired by flattery, as you know, nor by a motive of greed, God is witness.
2.6: We have sought no human glory from you or anyone else.
2.7: Although we, as apostles of Christ, could have claimed some authority, we were instead full of condescension among you. Like a nurse tenderly cares for her children,
2.8: Thus, in our affection for you, we would have liked to give you not only the Gospel of God, but our very lives, so dear had you become to us.
2.9: You remember, brothers, our labor and toil: we worked night and day, so that we would not be a burden to any of you, while we preached to you the gospel of God.
2.10: You are witnesses, and so is God, how holy, just, and blameless was our conduct toward you who believe.
2.11: As you know, we have been to each of you as a father is to his children,
2.12: praying, exhorting, and urging you to walk in a manner worthy of God, who calls you into his kingdom and glory.
2.13: Therefore, we also give thanks to God continually, because when you received the divine message, which we proclaimed to you, you accepted it not as a human word, but as it truly is, the word of God, which is also at work in you who believe.
2.14: For you, brothers, became imitators of God’s churches in Judea, which are gathered in Christ Jesus, since you also suffered at the hands of your fellow Jews as they did at the hands of the Jews,
2.15: of those Jews who put the Lord Jesus and the prophets to death, persecuted us, displease God, and are enemies of humankind,
2.16: hindering us from preaching salvation to the nations, so that they continually fill up the measure of their sins. But God's wrath has fallen upon them to remain with them until the end.
2.17: For us, brothers, sadly separated from you for a moment, in body, not in heart, we had a great eagerness and a keen desire to see you again.
2.18: We also wanted to come to you, especially I, Paul, a first and a second time, but Satan prevented us.
2.19: What, indeed, is our hope, our joy, our crown of glory? Is it not you who are in the presence of our Lord Jesus, on the day of his coming?
2.20: Yes, you are our glory and our joy.

3.1: So, unable to bear it any longer, we preferred to stay alone in Athens.,
3.2: And we have sent Timothy, our brother and God’s fellow servant in the gospel of Christ, to strengthen and encourage you in your faith,
3.3: so that no one would be weakened in the midst of these trials which, as you yourselves know, are our lot.
3.4: Even when we were with you, we predicted that we would face trials, which has indeed happened, as you know.
3.5: That is why, I too, unable to bear it any longer, sent to inquire about your faith, fearing that the tempter had tempted you and that our labor might be in vain.
3.6: But now that Timothy has just arrived here from you, he has told us of your faith and love and the fond memories you always have of us, and which make you long to see us again, just as we long to see you again,
3.7: So, brothers and sisters, in all our distress and affliction, we were comforted in you through your faith,
3.8: for now we live because you remain steadfast in the Lord.
3.9: Therefore, what thanksgiving can we offer to God for you, in the perfect joy we experience before our God because of you?.
3.10: Night and day we pray to Him with extreme fervor to grant us to see You and to complete what is still lacking in your faith.
3.11: May God himself, our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ, make our way to you straight.
3.12: And may the Lord increase and abound your charity towards one another and towards all people, as ours is towards you.
3.13: May he strengthen your hearts, making them blameless and holy before our God and Father, on the day when our Lord Jesus comes with all his saints, amen.

4.1: Finally, brothers, we ask and implore you in the Lord Jesus, since you have learned from us how to live in order to please God and in doing so, go on growing higher.
4.2: You are indeed familiar with the precepts that we gave you on behalf of the Lord Jesus,
4.3: For this is God’s will, your sanctification: that you avoid sexual immorality,
4.4: and that each of you knows how to keep your body in holiness and honor,
4.5: without abandoning it to the excesses of passion, as the pagans do who do not know God,
4.6: This is because no one in this matter uses violence or fraud against his brother, because the Lord brings justice to all these disorders, as we have already told you and attested to you.
4.7: For God did not call us to impurity, but to holiness.
4.8: Therefore, whoever despises these precepts does not despise a man, but God, who also gave his Holy Spirit to dwell in you.
4.9: As for brotherly love, there is no need to write to you about it, for you yourselves have been taught by God to love one another.
4.10: You should practice it well toward all the brothers throughout Macedonia. But we urge you, brothers, to practice it always better.
4.11: Strive to live peacefully, mind your own business, and work with your hands, as we have recommended.,
4.12: so that you might conduct yourself honestly in the eyes of outsiders, without needing anyone.
4.13: But we do not want you to be uninformed, brothers, about those who have fallen asleep, so that you do not grieve like the rest of mankind, who have no hope.
4.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, let us also believe that God will bring with Jesus those who have fallen asleep in him.
4.15: For this is what we declare to you according to the word of the Lord: We who are alive, left until the coming of the Lord, will not precede those who have fallen asleep,
4.16: For at the given signal, at the voice of the archangel, at the sound of the divine trumpet, the Lord himself will descend from heaven, and those who have died in Christ will rise first.
4.17: Then we who live, who are left, will be caught up with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will be with the Lord forever.
4.18: Therefore, comfort one another with these words.

5.1: As for the times and the moments, there is no need, brothers, to write to you about them.
5.2: For you yourselves know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
5.3: When men say, "Peace and security," then sudden destruction will come upon them like pain upon a woman in labor, and they will not escape it.
5.4: But you, brothers, are not in darkness, so that this day should surprise you like a thief.
5.5: Yes, you are all children of light and children of the day. We are not of the night, nor of the darkness.
5.6: Let us not sleep like the rest of men, but let us stay awake and be sober.
5.7: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, get drunk at night.
5.8: For we who belong to the day, let us be sober, putting on faith and love as a breastplate, and the hope of salvation as a helmet.
5.9: For God did not appoint us to wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
5.10: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep, we may live with him.
5.11: Therefore, comfort one another and build one another up, just as you are already doing.
5.12: We also ask you, brothers, to respect those who work hard among you, who govern you in the Lord, and who give you advice.
5.13: Show them greater charity because of their work. Live in peace with one another.
5.14: We beg you, brothers, rebuke those who disturb the peace, comfort those who are disheartened, support the weak, be patient with everyone.
5.15: Beware that no one should repay evil for evil, but always seek what is good for one another and for all.
5.18: In all circumstances give thanks, for this is God’s will in Christ Jesus for all of you.
5.21: But test everything and hold fast to what is good.
5.22: refrain from any appearance of evil.
5.23: May the God of peace himself sanctify you wholly, and may your whole spirit, soul and body be kept blameless until the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
5.24: The one who calls you is faithful, and he will do it again.
5.26: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
5.27: I adjure you by the Lord, that this letter be read to all the brothers. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
